questions.q: How many voting members are in the House of Representatives?
questions.explanation: There are 435 voting members, divided among the states by population.

questions.q: How are House seats divided among the states?
options: Equally — the same number for each state
By the year a state joined
questions.explanation: More populous states get more seats; every state gets at least one.

questions.q: How long is a term in the House?
questions.explanation: Short terms keep representatives accountable to voters.

questions.q: Which power belongs only to the House?
options: Starting revenue (tax) bills and impeaching officials
questions.explanation: Revenue bills begin in the House, which also impeaches officials.

questions.q: Who leads the House of Representatives?
questions.explanation: The Speaker leads the House and is second in line to the presidency.
